note:
大约一年前，我写了一篇关于我准备离开Substack的文章。我度过了假期，在WordPress上重建了网站。我在重新启动网站的过程中走得很远，然后在Substack与团队聊天并决定留下。
 一年后，我又回到了这里。这次，我与聪明的人合作，帮助我从构想变为执行。开关声名狼藉，一周后，我们来了。出于所有目的和目的，迁移似乎进展顺利。人们正在接收他们的时事通讯，对于想要存档的人来说，他们的登录情况也很好。 （如果您不在，请告诉我）。
  当其他所有人都涌向该平台，并且正经历着最好的几个月时，我为什么决定离开呢？首先，让我解释一下为什么Substack实际上是一个非常好的平台。
 当我第一次创建A Media Operator时，这是开始编写，发布和发布新闻简报的最简单方法，而这全是在我从事全职工作的时候。是的，我本可以设置自己的WordPress，然后发布并立即构建新闻通讯。我知道这样做的人。这很麻烦，需要时间，而且坦率地说，我想要更简单的东西。
 在2019年8月中旬，首次启动A Media Operator时，这是一个非常简单的主张：当我发表文章时注册并接收更新。我能够非常轻松地开始编写，发布和发送电子邮件。我对此感到高兴，说实话，我认为如果不是那样的话，今天就不会有媒体运营商。
 如果您要建立新闻简报/博客，目的是通过订阅获利，那么这可能是正确的方法。现在，您可以拥有自己的域名了，如果您只是建立基本的新闻通讯/博客，那实际上还不是那么糟糕。
 不过，媒体运营商已经成立一年半了，我开始将其视为不仅仅是新闻通讯。从长远来看，这会成为一家拥有多种收入来源的媒体公司吗？如果是这样，我是否需要能够支持这种发展的技术栈？ 
网站重新发布可能会令人讨厌。您必须清楚地知道需要什么与想要什么并确定优先级。尤其是当您进行精益操作时，必须非常集中精力。如果您不这样做，则将花费数年时间尝试构建某些东西，但仍然会陷入困境。
 我坐下来考虑了媒体运营商可以进入的各种商机：
 超高级社区：也许这只是一个高级主管团队。在从免费到付费的渠道中，这将低于付费。
 数据与研究：我应该开始建立有关公司筹集了多少资金，其收入，团队规模，盈利能力，业务模型等的数据库吗？
 咨询：我已经开始这样做，所以我的目标是明确提供它。
 高管对接会：我收到了很多人的提问，他们问：“您认识适合这个职位的人吗？”招聘人员赚钱。
 媒体工作室：整个夏天，我开始考虑媒体工作室的外观。我可以通过集中服务孵化并创立新的媒体公司吗？ 
活动：也许我为数百人发起了一个活动，获得了一些赞助商，并在Covid之后召集了人们。
  在寻找各种机会时，我意识到了一些非常重要的事情……我需要完全控制我的电子邮件列表。
 值得注意的是，使用Substack，您实际上不需要。从表面上看，您确实这样做了。您可以建立清单，离开时可以随身携带，也可以随时通过电子邮件发送。但是，这与完全控制我的电子邮件列表不同。
 此列表中有许多来自各种出版物的高级管理人员媒体运营商。名单上还包括记者，初级员工，个人创作者和其他各种各样的人。但是，当我准备发送时事通讯时，每个人都是平等的。
 进行细分时，我无法控制列表。假设我建立了一个超高级社区。我将如何营销？我显然不想给所有人发送电子邮件；甚至不是我的高级会员中的每个人。对他们来说可能不合适。我需要能够创建细分，而Substack不允许这样做。它是为新闻简报/博客而不是市场营销活动构建的。
 我非常感兴趣的另一件事是创建常绿内容以在Google中排名。我收到了一系列有关什么是ESP，DMP，CDP，首字母缩写词，人们应该考虑使用的东西等问题。我曾经想到的一个想法是，我可以创建常绿的内容，以挖掘所有这些工具，为搜索进行优化，然后从那里将用户转换为我的免费新闻通讯。
 子堆栈不支持该功能。每条内容都只是一篇博客文章，因此，我无法在网站上创建资源子目录。除此之外，我没有简单的方法可以对内容进行任何优化。这是基本的受众开发活动，我什至做不到。 
当我们谈论需要在Substack上发现更多内容的需求时，这就是我们在谈论的东西。让我优化我的内容。 Google为发布商带来了可笑的流量，但是却没有优先考虑的地方。
 我想启动的其他页面……联系页面，咨询页面，仅播客页面。我希望能够创建这些特定于特定业务需求的定制页面。
 关于受众群体发展，我已经考虑过创建推荐系统。我目前的老板为此创造了惊人的业务。我为什么不应该在这里呢？问题在于，由于Substack是一个封闭的花园，因此它没有可以与任何其他平台连接以支持奖励措施的API。
 对我来说越来越重要的另一件事是需要控制组订阅。这是一个动手过程。一家公司会来找我，说他们想让部分或全部员工成为会员。通常，此人已经是付费用户。我要么必须：
 退还该人的付费订阅费用，然后告诉他们第二次注册，但这次除外，请选择一个团体计划。
 请访问Substack的客户支持，要求他们处理，等待几周，然后仍然必须向用户说明该怎么做。
 也许我听起来有些恼火，但由于我对此过程感到尴尬，所以我暂停了$ 2,000的团体订阅。摩擦太大了，所以，我只是等待。我告诉他：“我正在移动系统，然后我们会做。” 
当我继续思考要构建的内容与Substack支持的内容时，我意识到自己陷入了困境。据我所知，我将永远无法建立一个超高级社区，封闭式数据库，创建分类的常绿内容，在站点上拥有一个事件页面，然后列表就不存在了。
 我已经到了尾巴摇狗的地步……工具决定了我的生意。
  做出决定的不只是产品需求。我还需要考虑媒体运营商代表什么，以及相反，Substack的理念。
  我写过的第一批文章之一就是关于多元化的重要性。如果一种收入来源枯竭，至少您可以获得另一种。
 对于整个“创造者经济”，我感觉甚至更多。每个人都应该获得有偿新闻通讯的想法很奇怪。某些内容应直接付款，而其他内容应用作间接获利的工具。我知道创作者可以免费写作，而仅通过咨询就可以获得可靠的六位数的业务。
 更明确地说，我想其中许多创作者将意识到在广告中可以赚钱。只需看看Packy在“无聊”中的构想即可。他的名单已经增加到25,000，并且通过销售非常好的广告获得了可观的收入。
 我已经看到创作者的发展。当他们第一次进入Substack时，他们对广告的糟糕程度表示敬意，并认为这会扭曲他们为读者制作的产品。快进了六个月，突然之间，他们意识到广告实际上是一个完全可行的选择，尤其是如果您以干净的方式进行广告。 
另一方面，子堆栈从根本上反对广告。这不是什么新鲜事。但这让我感到奇怪……Substack是否反对广告，我告诉我认识的所有人（酒吧和创作者）他们应该考虑广告策略，我是否与Substack在哲学上没有冲突？如果是，为什么要在该平台上发布？
 我并不是说Substack需要构建类似广告服务器的工具或类似工具。但是，一种简单的方法是计算链接的点击次数而不是点击百分比呢？那太好了。
 虽然不是真正的哲学，但我认为Substack需要解决一个更为关键的问题……谁是它的客户？
 当您来到媒体运营商时，您就是我的客户。然后，我成为Pico的客户，Pico是我为AMO业务提供动力的CRM /注册系统。但是您实际上不是Pico的客户。我付给Pico，您付给我，这种关系非常清楚。我不能对Substack说同样的话。
 您可以从他们的产品开发中了解很多有关企业客户的信息。随着新功能的推出，他们将从中受益？对于Substack，我不会感觉到它知道客户是谁，因此，其产品开发似乎有些偶然。
 以最近发布的阅读器应用为例。在Decoder的一集中，首席执行官Chris Best说：
 我们想到的很多事情是读者告诉我们的：“嘿，我现在已经订阅了六个不同的子堆栈，我想全部阅读它们，很糟糕的是它们与我的所有其他物品都在我的电子邮件收件箱中。 
“如果能有一个地方可以看到我拥有的所有这些直接联系，并且在很多情况下我都愿意为此付费，那不是很好吗？”这是我们非常感兴趣的事情。
 坦率地说，他们正在构建Google阅读器。这很酷。我们许多人希望Google阅读器仍然存在。除此以外，还有一个问题：此工具对创作者有何帮助？我不知道怎么做。它服务于其他客户，而不是创建新的媒体公司可能需要发展的工具。
 考虑一下Shopify。他们从“武装叛军”的立场行动。他们的顾客是商店的老板，他们花费所有的时间竭尽所能来帮助该顾客赚更多的钱。
 Substack构建了一个很棒的工具，用于发送新闻稿，发布博客甚至主持播客。但是后来它停了下来。它从未涉足构建用于受众开发的工具。它把重点转移到为读者服务上。很好，但是对于您的主要客户（创建者）而言，迭代很慢，但这并不是一件好事。
  首先，媒体运营商是一家数字媒体公司。但是，当记者们写这篇文章时，他们会将其称为“子堆栈”，例如博客或网站。我也不相信工具的品牌与我的品牌相同。听起来有些微不足道，但多个品牌却令人困惑。
 其次，因为他们的品牌是如此重要和中心，所以我必须非常警惕保护我的利益。我最近偶然发现了Substack上的一本铁杆右翼的新纳粹出版物。我不知道它是否仍然存在，但是如果人们打算将我的网站称为“子堆栈”，那么该平台上将会出现新纳粹分子，这可能会损害我的品牌。
  当我上周宣布更换产品时，我分解了所使用的工具： 
这些是为我的媒体公司提供动力的五种工具。但是，我将第一个告诉您我不是开发人员。早在八月，我在推特上说我正在寻找人，很幸运能邀请A Media Operator的成员伸出援手。
 本·梅（Ben May）是The Code Company的董事总经理，该公司专门致力于基于WordPress的数字出版商。他们的大多数客户是澳大利亚的大型媒体公司。但是我与他合作的原因是他了解我正在尝试建立的东西。因为他读过《媒体运营商》，所以我不必花时间分解我的工作。
 与Ben的过程很简单。我向他介绍了我长期考虑的功能。我们讨论了一些我特别喜欢的设计风格，然后他离开了，让他的团队模拟媒体运营商的模样。然后，他的团队构建了该网站，运行了一个基本脚本，以将我的内容发布到Substack上到WordPress（这并不像您想象的那么容易），并且在您不知道它的情况下，网站就完成了。
 我对Ben说的一件事是，我希望该业务的运营与一键式发布/发送时事通讯在Substack上的运行相同。他想出了如何将WordPress连接到Mailchimp，以便当我在WordPress中点击发布时，它也会发送新闻通讯。
 与Ben进行的对话中很大一部分是我们应该如何管理订阅。我们研究了很多系统。在反复讨论今天的业务和发展方向之后，我们同意Pico是适合我的工具。
 从本质上讲，Pico确实做到了两件事：它存储用户数据，并允许您创建各种订阅包。我可以根据用户来自何处创建规则，可以管理组订阅，实际上可以专注于建立业务。
 这并不意味着Pico是完美的工具。我不喜欢这些表格。我想要对样式和用户流程进行更多控制。我不是JS支持注册系统的最大支持者，因为它会降低运行速度。但是，我已经看到了即将推出的V2的外观。我将获得更多所需的自定义内容。 CRM将变得更加强大。我已经与团队就路线图进行了交谈，并且我相信当我准备开始向A Media Operator添加更多复杂功能时，Pico将可以为我提供支持。 
Pico知道其客户是谁。 读者可能会通过Pico的注册系统，但最终，我是Pico的付费用户。 这意味着它将继续构建使我成长的工具。  看...子堆栈很棒。 它做了我当时需要做的事情。 但是我对这家公司的野心远不止于此。 因此，我与The Code Co的Ben和Pico的Jason进行了合作。 我为未来感到兴奋。  如果您想加入A Media Operator的下一阶段并获得更多，请考虑升级为高级会员。 您可以在这里进行。
note_en:
It was almost a year ago that I wrote a piece about me getting ready to leave Substack. I was spending the holiday season rebuilding the site on WordPress. I got pretty far in the site relaunch and then chatted with the team over at Substack and decided to stay.
 A year later, I’m back here again. This time, I partnered with smart people to help me go from idea to execution. A week after the proverbial flipping of the switch, here we are. For all intents and purposes, it appears that the migration has gone pretty well. People are receiving their newsletter and, for those that want the archive, they are logging in just fine. (If you’re not, please let me know).
  When everyone else is rushing to the platform and it’s experiencing some of its best months yet, why did I decide to leave? To start, let me explain why Substack is actually a pretty good platform to use.
 When I first started A Media Operator, it was the easiest way to start writing, publishing and pushing out a newsletter—all while I was working a full-time job. Yes, I could have set up my own WordPress, published and then immediately built the newsletter. I know people that do that. It’s a hassle, takes time and, frankly, I wanted something simpler.
 In mid-August 2019, when A Media Operator was first booted up, it was a very simple proposition: sign up and receive updates when I publish essays. I was able to very easily start writing, publishing and emailing. I was happy about that and, honestly, I don’t think A Media Operator would be where it is today if it had not been for that.
 If you’re building a newsletter/blog with the goal of monetizing through subscriptions, it could be the right approach. And now that you can have your own domain name, it’s really not all that bad—if you’re  just building a basic newsletter/blog.
 A Media Operator is a year and a half old, though, and I had started to think about it as more than just a newsletter. In the long-term, could this become a media company with multiple streams of revenue? If so, won’t I need a technology stack that can support that evolution?
  Website relaunches can be irritating. You have to be crystal clear about what you need versus what you want and prioritize. Especially when you’re running a lean operation, being incredibly focused is imperative. If you don’t, you’ll spend years trying to build something and still be stuck at square one.
 I sat down and thought through the various business opportunities that A Media Operator could enter:
 Ultra-premium community: Maybe it’s just a c-suite group. In the funnel from free to paid, this would be below paid.
 Data & Research: Should I start building out databases of how much companies have raised, their revenue, team sizes, profitability, business models, etc.?
 Consulting: I had already started doing this, so my goal would be to explicitly offer it.
 Executive matchmaking: I’ve received quite a few questions from people asking, “do you know anyone that might fit this role?” Recruiters make solid money.
 Media studio: Over the summer, I started thinking about what a media studio might look like. Could I incubate and launch new media companies with centralized services?
 Events: Maybe I launch an event for a few hundred people, get some sponsors and bring people together post-Covid.
  As I was looking at a variety of these opportunities, I realized something incredibly important… I needed to have complete control of my email list.
 Remarkably, with Substack, you actually don’t. On the surface, it appears that you do. You build the list, you can take it with you when you leave and can email them whenever you want. However, that is different than having complete control of my email list.
 There are many c-suite media operators on this list from various types of publications. There are also journalists, junior-level employees, individual creators and a variety of other people on the list. But when I was preparing to send a newsletter, everyone was created equal.
 I had no control over the list when it came to segmentation. Let’s say I built that ultra-premium community. How would I market it? I obviously wouldn’t want to email everyone; not even everyone on my premium membership. It might not be right for them. I would need the ability to create a segment and Substack doesn’t allow that. It’s built for newsletter/blogs, not marketing operations.
 Another thing that I was very interested in was creating evergreen content to rank in Google. I receive a bunch of questions around what ESPs, DMPs, CDPs, acronym this, that and whatever people should be looking at using. One idea I’ve had was that I could create evergreen content that digs into all of these tools, optimize them for search and then convert users to my free newsletter from there.
 Substack doesn’t support that. Every piece of content is just a blog post, so it’s not like I could create a resource subdirectory on the site. Besides that, there was no simple way for me to do any optimization to the content. This is a basic audience development thing and I couldn’t even do it.
 When we talk about our need for content to be more discoverable on Substack,  this is something we’re talking about. Let me optimize my content. Google drives publishers a ridiculous amount of traffic and yet there was no priority there.
 Other pages I wanted to launch… a contact page, a consulting page, a podcast-only page. I wanted to be able to create these bespoke pages that were unique to a specific business need.
 On the topic of audience development, I’ve thought about creating a referral system. My current employer has built an amazing business with this. Why shouldn’t I do that here? The problem is that because Substack is a closed garden, it has no APIs that could connect with any other platform to support the incentives.
 Another thing that was increasingly becoming important to me was the need to control group subscriptions. This is a hands-on process. A company will come to me and say they want to get some or all of their employees a membership. Typically, the person is already a paying subscriber. I would either have to:
 Refund that person for their paid subscription and then tell them to sign up a second time, except this time, pick a group plan.
 Go to Substack’s customer support, ask them to handle it, wait a couple weeks and then still have to explain to the user what to do.
 Perhaps I sound a little irritated, but I put on hold a $2,000 group subscription because I was embarrassed about the process. The friction was too much and so, I simply waited. “I’m moving systems,” I told him, “and then we’ll do it.”
 As I continued to think through what I was trying to build versus what Substack was supporting, I realized I was stuck. As far as I could tell, I would never be able to build an ultra-premium community, gated databases, create categorized evergreen content, have an event page on the site and the list goes on.
 I had reached the point where the tail was wagging the dog… the tool was dictating my business.
  It wasn’t just product needs that contributed to my decision. I also needed to think about what A Media Operator stood for and, conversely, the philosophy of Substack.
  One of the first pieces I ever wrote was about the importance of diversification. In the event that one revenue stream dries up, at least you’ve got another.
 I feel that even more when it comes to the whole “creator economy.” This notion that everyone should have a paid newsletter is bizarre. Some content should be paid for directly while other content should be used as a tool to monetize indirectly. I know creators who write for free and earn a solid six-figure business just consulting.
 To be more explicit, I imagine many of these creators are going to realize that there is good money to be made in advertising. Just look at what Packy’s building over at Not Boring. He has grown his list to 25,000 and is earning a healthy income selling a very benign advertisement.
 I’ve seen the evolution of creators. When they first go on Substack, they pontificate about how advertising is bad and that it would distort the product they’re creating for the reader. Fast forward six months and suddenly, they realize that advertising is actually a perfectly viable alternative, especially if you do it in a clean way.
 Substack, on the other hand, is fundamentally opposed to advertising. This is nothing new. But it made me wonder… if Substack is so against advertising and I am telling everyone I know—pubs and creators—that they should be thinking about an ad strategy, am I not in philosophical conflict with Substack? And if I am, why am I publishing on that platform?
 I’m not saying Substack would need to build ad server-like tools or anything like that. But how about a simple means of counting the number of clicks on a link rather than just percent clicks? That’d be pretty sweet.
 While not truly philosophical, I believe there is a far more critical problem that Substack needs to account for… who is its customer?
 When you come to A Media Operator, you are my customer. I am then the customer to  Pico, the CRM/registration system I chose to power AMO’s business. But you’re not actually Pico’s customer. I pay Pico, you pay me, the relationship is crystal clear. I can’t say the same for Substack.
 You can learn a lot about who the customer of a business is based on their product development. As new features are introduced, who do they benefit? For Substack, I don’t get the feeling that it knows who its customer is and, therefore, its product development looks a little haphazard.
 Take, for example, the recently announced reader app. On an episode of  Decoder, CEO Chris Best said:
 Something that we think about a lot is readers tell us, ‘Hey, I’m subscribed to six different Substacks now, and I want to read them all, and it sucks that they’re in my email inbox along with all my other stuff.
 ‘Wouldn’t it be nice if there was one place where I could go and see all of these direct connections that I have, and that I’m paying for in many cases?’ That’s something that we’re keenly interested in.
 To be flippant, they’re building Google Reader. That’s cool. Many of us wish Google Reader still existed. Except, here’s a question: how does this tool help to creator? I don’t see how it does. Rather than creating the tools a new media company might need to thrive, it’s serving a different customer.
 Consider Shopify for a moment. They operate from the position of “arming the rebels.” Their customer is the shop owner and they spend all their time doing whatever they can to help that customer make more money.
 Substack built a great tool for sending a newsletter, publishing a blog and even hosting a podcast. But then it sort of stopped. It never dove into building tools for audience development. It pivoted its focus to serving the reader. That’s fine, but slow iteration for your primary customer—the creator—is just not great business.
  First, A Media Operator is a digital media company. But when journalists would write about it, they would refer to it as a “Substack” like blog or website. I also don’t believe in a tool’s brand being equal to my brand. It might sound petty, but multiple brands just confuse.
 Second, because their brand is so front and center, I have to be incredibly vigilant about protecting mine. I recently stumbled upon a hardcore, rightwing, Neo-Nazi publication on Substack. I don’t know if it is still there, but if people are going to refer to my site as a “Substack” and then there are going to be Neo-Nazis publishing on the platform, that could potentially hurt my brand.
  When I announced the change-over last week, I broke down the tools that I am using:
  Those are the five tools that power my media company. But I’ll be the first to tell you that I am not a developer. Back in August, I tweeted that I was looking for someone and I was fortunate enough to have a member of A Media Operator reach out.
 Ben May is the managing director at  The Code Company, an agency dedicated to digital publishers building on WordPress. Most of their clients are very large media companies in Australia. But the reason I partnered with him was that he understood what I was trying to build. Because he read A Media Operator, I didn’t have to spend time breaking down what I do.
 The process with Ben was straightforward. I walked him through the features I was thinking about over the long-term. We discussed some design styles that I particularly liked and then he went off and had his team mockup what A Media Operator could look like. His team then built the site, ran a basic script to get my content published on Substack over to WordPress (not as easy as you’d think) and before you know it, the site was done.
 One thing I said to Ben was that I wanted the operations of the business to run the same as it had on Substack vis-à-vis one-click publishing/sending newsletter. He figured out how to connect WordPress to Mailchimp so that when I hit publish in WordPress, it sends the newsletter as well.
 A big part of the conversation with Ben was how we should manage the subscriptions. We looked at a lot of systems. After going back and forth over where the business was today and where it was headed, we agreed that Pico was the right tool for me.
 At the core of it, Pico does two things really well… it stores user data and it allows you to create various subscription packages. I can create rules based on where users are coming from, I can manage my group subscriptions and I can actually focus on building my business.
 That doesn’t mean Pico’s the perfect tool. I’m not in love with the forms. I want more control over the style and user flow. I’m not the biggest fan of JS to power registration systems because it can slow things down. However, I’ve seen what the upcoming V2 looks like. I’m going to get more of the customization that I am looking for. The CRM is going to become more powerful. I’ve chatted with the team about the roadmap and I am confident that when I am ready to start adding more of the complicated functionality to A Media Operator, Pico will be in a place to support me.
 Pico knows who its customer is. Readers might flow through Pico’s registration system, but at the end of the day, I’m the one paying Pico. And that means that it will continue to build tools that make it possible for  me to grow.
 Look… Substack was great. It did what I needed it to do at the time. But my ambitions for this company are greater than that. And so, I partnered with Ben at The Code Co and Jason at Pico to make this work. I’m excited for the future.
 If you want to hop on board the next stage of A Media Operator and get even more, consider upgrading to a premium member.  You can do so here.
title: 数字媒体新闻发布者讨论了他离开Substack的产品和哲学原因，包括对电子邮件列表的控制不足
title_en: A publisher of newsletters on digital media discusses the product and philosophical reasons he is leaving Substack, including lack of control over email lists

西西里人开始于：西西里人在大师级别很受欢迎。布莱克立即争夺中心地位，但通过从c档攻击（而不是镜像怀特的举动），他创造了一个不对称的位置，从而导致许多复杂的位置。布莱克经常通过f6的骑士和b7的主教来攻击白色的电子棋子。布莱克想采取行动。没有报应。
 西西里人已经进行了广泛的研究，并且有很多变体。流行的变体是＆＃34; Dragon＆＃34;变化，开始于：1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 g6
 在这个变体中，Black finachettos在h8-a1对角线上有一个主教。这就是＆＃34; Dragon＆＃34;因为布莱克的典当结构看起来像龙，所以它的变化很大。
 颇受欢迎的另一个变体是Najdorf变体。它像龙一样开始，并在布莱克的移动5上发散：1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 a6
 据大师丹尼尔·金·怀特（Daniel KingWhite）经常以＆＃34; Be2＆＃34;做出回应，允许布莱克（Black）以＆＃34; e5！＆＃34;攻击中心。
  在法国国防部，布莱克让怀特对中心进行更多控制，以换取他建立（希望）安全的棋子墙。法国国防部的开始是：游戏通常涉及争夺位置。中心通常关闭，出现两个相互竞争的棋子链，每个玩家试图超越对方。布莱克（Black）尝试玩c5或f6。布莱克皇后（Bishop）经常变得被困和毫无用处，被称为``法国主教''（``Bishop''）。
  Caro-Kann就像法国的防御者一样-Black让White掌握了中心控制权，Black试图在d5处得到一个棋子，看起来像是一个``w弱的西西里人''。卡洛·坎恩的主线是布莱克（Black）可以消灭怀特（White）的中央典当之一，并且可以使他的棋子得到发展，这比法国国防部更具优势。被动的防守角色，因此此空缺的球员经常寻找White犯错误（但轻微）。 
开头有各种名称，例如＆＃34; Pirc＆＃34;和＆＃34; Modern＆＃34;。它开始于：或Keene标记了“现代防御”。作为顺序：这是一个相对较新的开场.1930年代，这被认为是次等的，但到1960年代，它被认为是相当可玩的。黑使怀特（White）成为中心，以破坏和破坏怀特（＆＃34） ;精彩＆＃34;这个开孔很难打，正确的打法是违反直觉的（立即中控不是目标，因为布莱克正试图破坏该控制）。
  现在我们来看＆＃34; 1以外的其他开口。 e4.The Queen's Gambit首先是：White提供了一个棋子以换取快速发展。Black可以用dxc4接受这个游戏，Queen's Gambit Accepted布莱克还可以玩Nc6（Tchigoran防御），e6（通向Tarrasch防御）或e6（正统防御）。
  这是一个“超现代”开幕式上，布莱克让怀特（White）成为中心人物，以期以后破坏怀特（Wonderful）的美景位置。这是一个冒险的空缺，是卡斯帕罗夫和菲舍尔的最爱。黑方会喜欢玩c5，而白方会玩d5，则回复e6和b5。
  所有这些＆＃34;印度＆＃34;防御始于：Nimzo-印地安人继续使用＆＃34; Nc3 Bb4＆＃34;。在Nimzo-印地安人中，怀特试图创建一个棋子中心并在其后面集中他的棋子进行攻击。
  荷兰人的防守始于：荷兰人的防守是布莱克的积极反击。布莱克立即开始向怀特的王室迈进，企图粉碎怀特。但是，从一开始，它也会在布莱克的位置上造成弱点-F兵的这一举动削弱了布莱克的防御能力，也无助于发展碎片。

note_en:
The Sicilian starts as: The Sicilian is popular at the master level.Black immediately fights for the center, butby attacking from the c-file (instead of mirroringWhite's move) he creates an asymmetrical positionthat leads to lots of complicated positions.Black tries to attack White's e-pawn, often through a Knight at f6 andBishop at b7. Black would like to make the move "d5" without retribution.
 The Sicilian has been extensively studied, and there are many variations.A popular variation is the "Dragon" variation, which starts as: 1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 g6
 In this variation, Black finachettos a bishop on the h8-a1 diagonal.This is called the "Dragon" variation because Black's pawn structureis supposed to look like a dragon.
 Another variation that's quite popular is the "Najdorf"variation. It starts just like the Dragon, anddiverges on Black's move 5: 1. e4 c5 2. Nf3 d6 3. d4 cxd4 4. Nxd4 Nf6 5. Nc3 a6
 According to Grandmaster Daniel KingWhite often responds with "Be2", permittingBlack to attack the center with "e5!".
  In the French Defense, Black lets White have more control over thecenter, in exchange for which he builds a (hopefully) safe wall of pawns.The French Defense starts as: Games generally involve jockeying for position. The center usually becomesclosed, two competing pawn chains arise, and each player tries to outflankthe other.White generally tries to play e5; Black tries to play c5 or f6.Black's queen Bishop often becomes trapped and useless, and it'sknown as the "French Bishop".
  The Caro-Kann is like the French defense - Black lets White build controlof the center, and Black tries to get a pawn at d5.It looks like a "wimpy Sicilian".The Caro-Kann starts out as: The main line of the Caro-Kann is  Black gets to eliminate one of White's central pawns and can gethis pieces developed, which is an advantage over the French Defense.However, Black's pieces end up with more of a passive defensive role,so players of this opening are often looking for White to makea mistake (however slight).
  This opening goes by various names, such as "Pirc" and "Modern". It starts: or Keene labels the "Modern Defense" as the sequence: This is a relatively new opening.In the 1930s this was considered inferior, but by the 1960s it wasfound to be quite playable.Black lets White take the centerwith the view to undermining and ruining White's "wonderful" position.This opening is tricky to play and correct play of it iscounter-intuitive (immediate center control is not a goal, sinceBlack is trying to undermine that control).
  Now we look at openings other than "1. e4".The Queen's Gambit starts with: White offers up a pawn in exchange for rapid development.Black can accept the gambit with dxc4, playing "Queen's Gambit Accepted",which is a risky way to play this gambit.Black can also play Nc6 (the Tchigoran Defense), e6 (which leads to theTarrasch Defense), or play e6 (the Orthodox Defense).
  This is a "hypermodern" opening, where Black lets White take the centerwith the view to later ruining White's "wonderful" position.It's a risky opening, a favorite of both Kasparov and Fischer. Black will be interested in playing c5, and when White plays d5,reply with e6 and b5.
  All of these "Indian" defenses start with: The Nimzo-Indian continues with "Nc3 Bb4".In the Nimzo-Indian, White tries to create a pawn center and mass hispieces behind behind them for attack.
  The Dutch defense starts as: The Dutch defense is an aggressive counterplay by Black.Black immediately begins to move toward White's kingside in an attemptto crush White. However, it also creates weaknesses in Black's positionfrom the beginning - this move of the f-pawn weakens Black's defensesand doesn't help develop pieces.
  The English opening is a "flank" manuever. It starts very differently: Here White hopes to control the center by first gaining support on the side.A common response for Black is "c5".
  How to Play the Opening in Chess.1993. Raymond Keene and David Levy.ISBN 0-8050-2937-0. The Encyclopedia of Chess Openings. Batsford Chess Openings 2.1989, 1994.Garry Kasparov and Raymond Keene.New York, New York: Henry Holt and Company.ISBN 0-8050-3409-9.

上周，在Docker Community All Hands期间，我们宣布了可通过Docker Developer Preview程序在M1上运行的Mac的Docker桌面开发人员预览版本的可用性。截止到今天，我们已经有1000多人在测试这些版本。如果您有兴趣加入该程序以获取将来的版本，则应该立即开始！
 如您所知，Apple最近已经发售了第一批基于新Apple M1芯片的Mac。上个月，我的同事Ben分享了我们构建在此新硬件上运行的Docker桌面的路线图。很高兴告诉您，今天我们有一个公开预览，您可以下载并试用。
 像你们中的许多人一样，我们在Docker上为使用这些新计算机进行接收和编码感到非常兴奋：它们感觉太快了！我们也知道Docker桌面是超过3M开发人员使用Docker桌面的开发周期的关键部分，而在您的Mac上有超过一半的人使用Docker桌面。为了支持我们所有的Mac用户，我们一直在努力使Docker Desktop准备在新的M1硬件上运行。它还不是发行质量，甚至不是beta质量，但是我们有一个早期的预览版本，我们希望让您尽快尝试。
  当Ben宣布我们正在努力在此新硬件上调整Docker Desktop时。为了向您发布此版本，我们面临大约3个工程挑战：
  Docker Desktop团队面临的主要挑战之一是用苹果提供的Virtualization Framework取代Docker于2016年开源的HyperKit，macOS Big Sur中包含了该虚拟化框架。
  到目前为止，我们在工具链中用于构建这些二进制文件的许多工具尚未准备好支持M1 Mac。在Docker，我们广泛使用Go语言，并且Docker Desktop也不例外。 Go语言将在其目标版本2021年2月的1.16版本中支持Apple Silicon。
  Docker桌面团队通过持续集成在很大程度上依赖于自动化测试，以确保我们发布的质量。在本周之前，由于我们的合作伙伴都没有足够的M1机器，因此无法建立持续集成。幸运的是，我们正在与MacStadium合作，并且正在我们的CI系统上设置新的M1 Mac。 
感谢我们在前两个步骤中所取得的重大进展，今天我们将分享M1的Docker桌面技术预览。在这里下载！
  许多开发人员将首次使用M1 Mac体验多平台开发。这是Docker大放异彩的关键领域之一。 Docker长期以来一直支持多平台映像，这意味着您现在可以在Desktop上构建和运行x86和ARM映像。 M1上的新Docker桌面也不例外。您可以为x86和Arm架构构建和运行映像，而无需设置复杂的交叉编译开发环境。
 Docker Hub还可以轻松识别和共享提供多平台映像的存储库。
  最后，使用docker buildx，您还可以轻松地将多平台构建集成到构建管道中。
  赶在年末庆祝活动的时候，我们很高兴与您分享我们的M1预览：
  请记住，这是一个预览版本：它可能会损坏，还没有像我们的正常版本和“龙在这里”那样经过全面测试。需要您的帮助才能在Apple Silicon上测试Docker桌面，以便我们可以继续在所有Apple设备上提供出色的开发人员体验。您可以通过在docker / for-mac上提供错误报告来帮助我们。我们将使用此反馈来帮助我们改进和迭代Desktop产品和多体系结构体验，因为我们的目标是在2021年第一季度提供Docker Desktop的GA构建。
 同时，享受Docker M1版的技术预览版。节日快乐！
note_en:
Last week, during the Docker Community All Hands, we announced the availability of a developer preview build of Docker Desktop for Macs running on M1 through the Docker Developer Preview Program. We already have more than 1,000 people testing these builds as of today. If you’re interested in joining the program for future releases you should  do it today!
 As I’m sure you know by now, Apple has recently shipped the first Macs based on the new Apple M1 chips. Last month my colleague Ben shared  our roadmap for building a Docker Desktop that runs on this new hardware. And I’m delighted to tell you that today we have a public preview that you can download and try out.
 Like many of you, we at Docker have been super excited to receive and code with these new computers: they just feel so fast! We also know that Docker Desktop is a key part of the development cycle for over 3M developers using Docker Desktop with over half of you on Macs. To support all our Mac users we’ve been working hard to get Docker Desktop ready to run on the new M1 hardware. It is not release quality yet, or even beta quality, but we have an early preview build and we wanted to let you try it as soon as possible.
  When  Ben announced that we were working on adapting Docker Desktop on this new hardware. We had roughly 3 engineering challenges to tackle to get this release out to you:
  One of the key challenges for the Docker Desktop team was to replace HyperKit, which Docker  open sourced back in 2016, with the   Virtualization Framework provided by Apple which was included in macOS Big Sur.
  Many of the tools that we use in our toolchain to build these binaries are not yet ready to support the M1 Mac as of today. At Docker, we use the Go language extensively, and Docker Desktop is no exception. The Go language will support Apple Silicon in their  1.16 release which is targeted for February 2021.
  The Docker Desktop team relies heavily on automated testing through continuous integration to ensure the quality of our releases. Until this week our continuous integration could not be set up because none of our partners had enough M1 machines yet. Fortunately, we are working with  MacStadium and we are setting up new M1 Macs on our CI system.
 Thanks to the significant progress we have been able to make on the first two steps, we are sharing a Tech Preview of Docker Desktop for M1 today.   Download it here!
  Many developers are going to experience multi-platform development for the first time with the M1 Macs. This is one of the key areas where Docker shines. Docker has had support for  multi-platform images for a long time, meaning that you can build and run both x86 and ARM images on Desktop today. The new Docker Desktop on M1 is no exception; you can build and run images for both x86 and Arm architectures without having to set up a complex cross-compilation development environment.
 Docker Hub also makes it easy to identify and share repositories that provide multi-platform images.
  And finally, using docker buildx you can also easily integrate  multi-platform builds into your build pipeline.
  Right on time for the year-end festivities, we’re excited to share with you our M1 Preview:
  Keep in mind that this is a preview release: it may break, it has not been tested as thoroughly as our normal releases and ‘here be dragons’. Your help is needed to test Docker Desktop on Apple Silicon so that we can continue to provide a great developer experience on all Apple devices. You can help us by providing bug reports on  docker/for-mac. We will use this feedback to help us improve and iterate on both the Desktop product and the multi-architecture experience as we aim to provide a GA build of Docker Desktop in the first quarter of 2021.
 In the meantime, enjoy this tech preview build of Docker Desktop for M1. Happy Holidays!
title: M1的Docker桌面技术预览
title_en: Tech Preview of Docker Desktop for M1

十三岁的约翰·科尔曼（John Coleman）竭尽全力购买了Sony PlayStation5。来自马里兰州鲍伊（Bowie）的科尔曼（Coleman）花了夏天的时间打扫和修剪草坪，以节省游戏机本来应该花的500美元。他一直待到凌晨5点，Target的第一批产品开始销售，并于黑色星期五在马里兰州GameStop的帐篷前扎营。 PS5首次亮相一个月后，他在虚拟学校上班后每天检查库存警报，但仍然没有控制台。
 47岁的Ted Brack在拉斯维加斯的两台电脑显示器前追赶新的PlayStation，结果却大相径庭。
 到目前为止，布拉克已经从包括沃尔玛在内的在线零售商那里购买了八种游戏机，并在eBay上以高达1,160美元的价格出售。他的秘密武器：机器人或软件，可以帮助他知道产品何时库存，并且可以以比任何普通客户自己希望的更快的速度锤击零售商。
    自动执行在线任务的计算机程序（称为bot）已经与冠状病毒大流行和热门产品的低库存保持一致，从而根据假期的观点，为假期带来了极大的失望或机会。在黑色星期五，当它在游戏机上发起交易时，沃尔玛网站表示，在销售的前30分钟内，它阻止了超过2000万次自动程序尝试。 Target表示，它一直在跟踪和阻止漫游器，专注于PS5等高需求产品。一家名为Very的英国零售商表示，在意识到它们被机器人放置后，至少取消了1000个游戏机订单。
 尽管零售商心急如焚，而且让科尔曼（Coleman）等顾客感到烦恼，但使用购物机器人购买这些产品在美国完全合法。一些机器人运营商是现代的黄牛，通过强迫圣诞老人支付市场价格来赚钱。其他人则是精通计算机的购物者，如今他们在绝望中转向机器人来填写自己的礼物清单。
  购物机器人不是新手，但其用途正在迅速增长。他们由购买和转售门票，高端运动鞋和名牌时装的人员部署，现在正扩展到供不应求的其他类别，包括大流行时期的杂货送货处。网络安全公司Imperva表示，在其客户中，“不良机器人”占2019年所有流量的24.1％，高于2018年的20.4％。（“优质机器人”类似于Google的搜索引擎侦察兵。）
  僵尸防护公司Netacea的电子商务负责人Thomas Platt说：“这是一场全面的军备竞赛，并且在不断升级。” 
机器人只是PS5危机的一部分-甚至还有大胆的抢劫。但是，在连接了许多不同利益的互联网经济中，说停止使用僵尸程序比说起来容易做起来难得多：想要生产备受追捧的产品的公司，以及愿意采取任何措施来获取它们的早期采用者。零售商主要投资于转向库存和希望减少销售的在线转售市场。然后还有像布拉克（Brack），维加斯（Vegas）转售商这样的小型企业，以及制造他使用的机器人的人（通常是青少年）。
 “我明白为什么有人对此感到不高兴。但只要有需求，您总会在买卖双方之间找到人。”布拉克说。他说，他今年会从侧面赚到30,000美元。
  “我知道这是一种快速赚钱的方法，”现年13岁的科尔曼说。但是“想想一直在等待的小孩；这将是他们的第一个控制台。”
   2020年的PS5战役表明，即使是谁负责，也不清楚。
  布拉克整晚都在熬夜，然后在11月12日购买了他的首批4台PS5。他知道自己必须在太平洋时间正好上午9点准备好武器库，当时沃尔玛宣布第一批PS5将开始销售。
 这可能是沃尔玛在机器人操作员与猫和老鼠之间进行的持续游戏中的第一个错误：让他们确切知道何时该罢工。
 布拉克（Brack）三年前开始购买和转售限量版运动鞋，此后就一直在完善“警察”或成功购买的艺术。那天早上他的工具包括一个他花了250美元购买的机器人。他在弗吉尼亚州的虚拟服务器上运行该服务器，该服务器提供的连接速度比他在家中的笔记本电脑所能承受的速度更快。 
在机器人内部（一种看上去很像专业办公软件的桌面应用程序，但具有游戏玩家友好的深色），他在自己想要购买的产品上粘贴了一个链接，然后输入了信用卡。
  但是，“应对”热门产品并不像打开应用程序那样简单。零售商有许多防御措施，机器人用户需要解决。布拉克（Brack）操作代理来遮掩他的IP地址，甚至为他的送货地址创建了一些经过微调的版本，以避免出现多个可疑订单。
  零售商声称他们掌握了这些技巧。沃尔玛首席信息安全官杰里·盖斯勒（Jerry Geisler）表示：“机器人程序脚本不断发展和被重写，因此我们已经构建，部署并不断更新我们自己的机器人程序检测工具，从而使我们能够成功地阻止所看到的绝大多数机器人程序。” ，在一份声明中说。
  他还说，它对游戏机的大部分需求来自真实的人，并且该公司审核并取消了由机器人购买的订单，尽管他没有说多少沃尔玛取消了。盖斯勒说：“我们的绝大多数下一代游戏机已被合法客户购买。”
  沃尔玛并没有阻止布拉克。上午9点开始销售时，他的机器人开始工作了。到9:01时，应用程序中的两个图标从红色变为绿色-布拉克订购了两个PS5。那天下午，沃尔玛进行了第二次股票发行，他又买了两股。该零售商表示，其僵尸程序检测和预防工具在每个版本中均会更加有效。
 与零售商合作但未透露客户的Imperva表示，其软件给机器人程序带来了障碍，例如检查浏览器的200个属性以查看其是否为真实设备。应用程序安全策略师爱德华·罗伯茨（Edward Roberts）表示：“通过这种方式，如果它们越过一个障碍，那么您就可以像验证码一样放置东西来检测它们。”
  Captchas是“完全自动化的公共Turing测试以告诉计算机和人类的区别”的缩写，是应该检测机器人的测验。但是Brack的工具包也有解决这些问题的方法，包括一项订阅服务，该服务将验证码发给实时填写的人。 
进一步使战斗更加复杂的是，某些机器人仅提供信息，例如何时有库存，这对转售商而言比购买机器人更有用。
 布拉克的下四项PS5胜利归功于一家名为Fulcrum的公司提供的每月500美元的服务，该服务可为他提供库存中的即时更新以及隐藏在仅对搜索引擎和漫游器可见的部分零售商站点中。这就是他上周在Amazon上获得PS5补货的方式，无需借助机器人即可完成实际购买。
   正如沃尔玛所说，即使机器人仅占PS5销量的一小部分，显然，真正的需求量也远远超过了可用的新游戏机数量。如果真实和虚拟货架上的单位更多，则机器人操作员将无法进行大量转售。
   新的控制台每六到七年发布一次，比起更新频率更高的设备，它已经产生了更多的预期。但这只是今年大发行中的问题之一。大流行和社交活动的限制促使世界各地的人们蜂拥而至。市场研究公司IDC游戏研究总监Lewis Ward表示，这种流行病还扰乱了供应链。他说：“只有这么多硬件可以使用。”
  短缺的原因可能与流行病无关，这是公司不想立即售出太多产品的原因。
  IDC的Ward估计，索尼本季度将发售近500万台PS5，微软将出售380万台Xbox Series X，这是机器人购买的另一个热门项目。 （微软拒绝置评。）由于高端硬件组件的高昂成本，两家公司目前在每个游戏机上的利润甚微。 Ward说，随着零件成本的下降，未来几年游戏系统的制造成本将会降低，这将使公司未来出售的游戏机更有利可图。
   “他们在这些捆绑产品上亏损。他们有动力不要跑到最大数目。”沃德说，他指出游戏机的使用寿命可以达到12年。 “随着时间的推移，他们会从中赚更多的钱。” 
在线市场是Internet上最原始的，也是最赚钱的业务之一，它也使漫游器操作员可以轻松，方便地以高价出售商品。 Craigslist和Facebook Marketplace的审核很少，可以轻松地找到愿意在停车场现金付款的附近买家。
 布拉克在eBay上出售了他所有的PS5。他说：“说实话，我不认为他们介意。”
 他是对的。该平台具有广泛的规则和保护措施，以试图防止人们销售假冒产品，但没有任何规则禁止以其零售价的两倍转售机器人购买的商品。 eBay发言人艾希莉·塞特尔（Ashley Settle）表示：“允许将真实的控制台转售出去，如果列出正确，则不会被视为欺诈。”
  布拉克说，机器人操作员不像格林奇公司那样，而更像一个有侧边的人。
 “这只是我喜欢做的事情，”布拉克说。 “不仅仅是钱。”
 尽管Brack从事网络开发人员的工作是固定的，但他的收入中约有一半来自与机器人相关的企业，包括他经营的一个名为CopSupply的网站，该网站可以帮助人们比较机器人。他还为经销商开设了一个名为CopWorks Cook Group的Discord聊天室，在其中，他们每月支付30美元，以获取有关新产品和其他提示的信息。
 这些社区服务使他对机器人经济产生了鸟瞰，在这种情况下，他称自己为平庸的玩家。在过去的一年中，它很容易翻了一番。他说：“由于大流行，每个人都搬进了里面。” “我认为经济衰退将袭来，人们将不太倾向于购买产品。但是事实并非如此。这是拥有侧忙的完美方式。” 
安全公司还表示，有证据组看起来更像传统的黑客组织正在加入经济。 Netacea表示，在欧洲，只有一个很小但组织良好的转售机器人小组在头两周内从PS5获利超过100万美元。 Netacea电子商务负责人普拉特（Platt）说，看到有足够资金的机器人团体“是一个巨大的改变”。 “我们不习惯人们投资一百万美元购买股票。”
  安全公司表示，零售商和产品制造商需要通过使用更多软件来改变机器人市场的经济状况。 “他们需要使僵尸网络操作者的攻击成本更高。现在，它太便宜了，回报也太高了。” Imperva的罗伯茨（Roberts）说。
 美国正在考虑根据2016年《 BOTS法》制定法律，该法律规定，使用该软件剥头皮票是非法的。众议员保罗·通科（Paul N.他计划在2021年再次提出该法案。
 “为什么有人要操纵市场，家庭为什么要付出更高的代价？”通子在接受采访时说。 “那里的不公平现象似乎在加剧，它对当前大流行气氛和经济衰退的影响更加令人痛苦。”
 沃尔玛（Walmart）的盖斯勒（Geisler）周二在博客中表示，该公司正在要求立法者对机器人采取行动，并希望其他零售商也能加入。
 但是，仍然有待解决的细节包括：确切行为将是非法的，如何追究国际机器人运营商以及零售商必须向联邦贸易委员会报告机器人行为的责任，甚至是停止使用机器人自己检查价格的细节。在竞争对手上。
 在寻找PS5的过程中，许多定期购买者报告说，他们已经采用了一些从机器人获利者那里获得的技术，有机会击败他们-以及成千上万的其他购物者-结账。他们遵循@ Wario64之类的技巧帐户，发布有关商店何时提供库存的谣言，并在库存机器人的帮助下设置警报。为了避免浪费时间输入信用卡号和送货地址，许多公司提前与每个零售商创建了帐户。他们加入了Discord渠道，例如ScalperRevenge（拥有3,000多个会员），以交流技巧，并观看YouTube指导视频以及购买建议。 
马里兰州的少年科尔曼（Coleman）尝试了很多操作，包括库存警报和让父母在诸如Target，GameStop，Best Buy和Walmart等网站上建立帐户。 他设法将PS5放入购物车中，但是当他点击结帐时，该PS5便消失了，这是各个站点的购物者之间的共同抱怨。  在《华盛顿邮报》向布拉克征询对寻找PS5的少年科尔曼的评论后，布拉克表示他想向他发送他从机器人中获得的游戏机之一。 “我很难过，”他说。
note_en:
Thirteen-year-old John Coleman has tried everything to buy a  Sony PlayStation 5. Coleman, from Bowie, Md., spent his summer cleaning and mowing lawns to save up the $500 the game console is supposed to cost. He stayed up until 5 a.m. when Target’s first units went on sale, and camped in front of a Maryland GameStop on Black Friday. A month after the PS5’s debut, he checks inventory alerts every day after virtual school but still doesn’t have the console.
 Ted Brack, 47, chases down new PlayStations in front of two computer monitors in Las Vegas — with very different results.
 Brack has bought eight of the consoles so far from online retailers including Walmart, selling them for as much as $1,160 on eBay. His secret weapon: bots, or software that helps him know when products are in stock and can hammer retailers with orders faster than any regular customer could hope to on their own.
    Computer programs that automate online tasks, called bots, have aligned with the  coronavirus pandemic and low inventories of hot products to create a perfect storm of holiday disappointment — or opportunity, depending on your perspective. On Black Friday, when it launched a deal on the console, Walmart.com says it blocked more than 20 million bot attempts in the sale’s first 30 minutes. Target says it’s constantly tracking and blocking bots, focusing on high-demand products such as the PS5. One British retailer called Very said it canceled at least 1,000 game console orders after it realized they were placed by bots.
 Using shopping bots to buy these products is perfectly legal in the United States, despite flustering retailers and stoking annoyance for customers like Coleman. Some bot operators are modern scalpers, in it to make money by forcing Santa to pay market prices. Others are computer-savvy shoppers now turning to bots out of desperation to fill their own gift lists.
  Shopping bots aren’t new, but their use is growing fast. Deployed by people who buy and resell tickets, high-end sneakers and designer fashion, they’re now expanding into other categories where demand outstrips supply — including grocery delivery slots at the height of the pandemic. Imperva, a cybersecurity firm, says that among its clients, “bad bots” accounted for 24.1 percent of all traffic in 2019 — up from 20.4 percent in 2018. (“Good bots” are ones like Google’s search engine scouts.)
  “It’s a full-on arms race that keeps escalating,” says Thomas Platt, the head of e-commerce at bot-protection firm Netacea.
   Bots are only one part of the PS5 crunch — there have even been daring heists. But stopping the use of bots is easier said than done in an Internet economy that connects so many different interests: companies that want to make highly sought-after products and early adopters who will do anything to get them. Retailers primarily invested in turning inventory and online resale marketplaces hoping for a cut. And then there are small-business people like Brack, the Vegas reseller, and the people (often teenagers) who make the bots he uses.
 “I can see why somebody would get upset about it. But any time that there’s demand for something, you’re always going to find somebody in between a purchaser and seller,” says Brack, who says he’ll make about $30,000 this year from his side hustle.
  “I understand it’s a way to earn a lot of money quickly,” says Coleman, the 13-year-old. But “think about the little kids who’ve been waiting for it; it will be their first console.”
   The PS5 battles of 2020 show it isn’t exactly clear who, even, is responsible.
  Brack was up all night before he purchased his first four PS5s on Nov. 12. He knew he had to have his arsenal ready at exactly 9 a.m. Pacific time, when Walmart had announced its first bunch would go on sale.
 That was probably Walmart’s first mistake in an ongoing game of cat and mouse with bot operators: letting them know exactly when to strike.
 Brack got his start three years ago buying and reselling limited-edition sneakers, and has since been perfecting the art of the “cop,” or successful purchase. His tools that morning included a bot he bought for $250. He ran it on a virtual server in Virginia, which offered a faster connection than he could muster from his laptop at home.
  Inside the bot — a desktop application that looks a lot like professional workplace software, but with gamer-friendly dark colors — he pasted a link to the product he wanted to buy and entered his credit card.
  But “copping” hot products isn’t as simple as just switching on an app. Retailers have a number of defenses in place that bot users need to work around. Brack operates proxies to obscure his IP address, and even created slightly tweaked versions of his shipping address, to avoid having multiple orders look suspicious.
  Retailers claim they are onto these tricks. “Bot scripts are constantly evolving and being re-written, so we’ve built, deployed and are continuously updating our own bot detection tools allowing us to successfully block the vast majority of bots we see,” Jerry Geisler, Walmart’s chief information security officer,  said in a statement.
  He also said most of its demand for the consoles is from real people and that the company audits and cancels orders purchased by bots, though he didn’t say how many Walmart had canceled. “The vast majority of our next-gen consoles have been purchased by legitimate customers,” Geisler said.
  Walmart didn’t stop Brack. When sales began at 9 a.m., his bot went to work. By 9:01, two icons turned from red to green inside the app — and Brack had two PS5s ordered. That afternoon, Walmart did a second stock release, and he bought two more. The retailer says its bot detection and prevention tools get more effective with each release.
 Imperva, which works with retailers but doesn’t disclose its clients, says its software puts up obstacles to bots, such as checking for 200 attributes of the browser to see if it’s a real device. “That way if they trip over one obstacle, then you put something like a captcha in the way to detect them,” said Edward Roberts, an application security strategist.
  Captchas, short for “Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart,” are the quizzes that are supposed to detect bots. But Brack’s tool kit has ways around those, too, including a subscription service that farms out Captchas to humans who fill them out in real time.
  Complicating the fight further, some bots just provide information such as when something is in stock — which can be even more useful for resellers than a purchasing bot.
 Brack’s next four PS5 wins came thanks to a $500-a-month service from a company called Fulcrum that gives him instant updates on what’s in stock and hidden on parts of retailer sites visible only to search engines and bots. That’s how he caught a PS5 restock on Amazon just last week, completing the actual purchase without the aid of a bot.
   Even if bots account for only a fraction of PS5 sales, as Walmart claims, there is clearly a massive amount of genuine demand outstripping the number of new game consoles available. If there were more units on real and virtual shelves, bot operators wouldn’t be able to make so much reselling them.
   New consoles come out once every six to seven years, which already creates more anticipation than more frequently updated devices. But that was only one of the issues for this year’s big releases. The pandemic and limits on social activities have pushed people around the world toward gaming in droves. The pandemic has also disrupted supply chains, according to Lewis Ward, research director of gaming at market research firm IDC, who says “there’s only so much hardware to go around.”
  There’s probably another reason for the shortage that isn’t related to the pandemic: The companies don’t want to sell too many units right away.
  IDC’s Ward estimates Sony will ship nearly 5 million PS5s this quarter, and Microsoft will move about 3.8 million Xbox Series Xs, another hot item for bot-buying. (Microsoft declined to comment.) The companies are probably making very little profit on each console at the moment, if at all, due to the high cost of their high-end hardware components. But the game systems will get cheaper to make in the coming years as the cost of parts falls, making consoles sold in the future more profitable for the companies, says Ward.
   “They are losing money on these bundles. They have incentive not to run up to a giant number,” says Ward, who points out that consoles can have a 12-year life span. “They’ll make more money on them over time.”
 Online marketplaces, one of the Internet’s original and most profitable businesses, also make it very easy and convenient for bot operators to sell their goods at a significant markup. Craigslist and Facebook Marketplace have minimal moderation and make it simple to find nearby buyers willing to pay in cash in a parking lot.
 Brack sold all of his PS5s on eBay. “I don’t think they mind, to be honest,” he said.
 He’s right. The platform has extensive rules and protections to try to prevent people from selling fake products, but no rules against reselling bot-purchased merchandise for twice its retail price. “The reselling of real consoles is allowed and is not considered fraudulent if they are listed correctly,” said eBay spokeswoman Ashley Settle.
  Brack says bot operators are less like the Grinch and more like people with a side hustle.
 “It’s just something I happen to love doing,” Brack said. “It’s not about just the money.”
 While Brack has a regular job as a Web developer, about half of his income comes from bot-related enterprises, including a website he runs called  CopSupply that helps people comparison shop for bots. He also runs a Discord chat room called  CopWorks Cook Group for resellers, in which they pay $30 per month to get intel on new products and other tips.
 Those community services have given him a bird’s-eye view on the bot economy, in which he calls himself a mediocre player. Over the last year, it has easily doubled. “Everybody moved inside because of the pandemic,” he says. “I thought that a recession would hit and people would be less inclined to buy products. But that’s just not the case. This is the perfect way to have a side hustle.”
 Security companies also say there’s evidence groups that look more like traditional hacking outfits are joining the economy. Netacea says just one small but well-organized resale bot group in Europe made a profit of over $1 million in the first two weeks from the PS5. Seeing bot groups that well-funded “is a big change,” said Platt, Netacea’s head of e-commerce. “We are not used to people investing a million dollars to buy up stock.”
  Security firms say retailers and product makers need to change the bot market economics by using more of their software. “They need to make the attacks more expensive for the bot operator. Right now, it’s too cheap and the rewards are too high,” says Imperva’s Roberts.
 The United States is considering legislation based on the 2016 BOTS Act, which made it illegal to use the software to scalp tickets. Rep. Paul Tonko (D-N.Y.) sponsored that legislation, and in 2018 proposed the Stopping Grinch Bots Act, which would empower the Federal Trade Commission to sue people who use bots to circumvent anti-bot protections from all kinds of retailers. He plans to introduce the bill again in 2021.
 “Why should families have to pay a higher price because someone has manipulated the market?” Tonko said in an interview. “There’s an unfairness there that seems to have grown — and its impact in the current pandemic atmosphere and the economic downturn is all the more painful.”
 Walmart’s Geisler said in a blog post Tuesday that the company was asking lawmakers to take action against bots and hoped other retailers would join it.
 Still to be worked out, however, are the details around which exact behavior would be illegal, how to go after international bot operators and what responsibility retailers would have to report bot behavior to the FTC — or even to stop using bots themselves to check prices on competitors.
 In the hunt for PS5s, plenty of regular buyers reported having adopted some of the techniques from bot profiteers to stand a chance of beating them — and the thousands of other human shoppers — to checkout. They follow tip accounts such as @Wario64, which tweets out rumors about what times stores will make stock available, and they set up alerts with the help of inventory bots. Many create accounts with each retailer ahead of time, to avoid wasting time entering a credit card number and shipping address. They join Discord channels such as ScalperRevenge (with over 3,000 members) to swap tips and watch YouTube how-to videos with advice on buying.
 Coleman, the teenager in Maryland, has tried much of it, including inventory alerts and having his parents set up accounts on sites such as Target, GameStop, Best Buy and Walmart. He has managed to get a PS5 in his shopping cart, but it disappears by the time he hits checkout — a common complaint among shoppers across sites.
 After The Washington Post asked Brack for comment on Coleman, the teenager hunting for the PS5, Brack said he wanted to send him one of the consoles he had obtained with his bot. “I feel bad,” he said.

 该市监事会的一个小组对此提出了谴责，此举反映了科技行业及其创始人之间不断争议的新政治。左右的激进主义者对Facebook等大型科技公司的批评越来越强烈。同时，人们对超级富豪的慈善礼物发起了强烈反对。
 3-0的投票代表了这些错流中的每一个，在自由派旧金山这两个流都特别强烈。该措施没有法律效力。但是，预计将于12月15日举行董事会的象征性全票。
 五年前，扎克伯格和他的妻子普丽西拉·陈（Priscilla Chan）向该市唯一的公立医院旧金山总医院捐赠了7500万美元，当时，陈是该医院的儿科医生。作为捐赠的一部分，该医院正式更名为Priscilla Chan和Mark Zuckerberg旧金山总医院和创伤中心。
 此后，随着Facebook规模的扩大，扎克伯格已经成为政治上的骗子，并被一系列丑闻所困扰。扎克伯格在公司生活中遇到的麻烦越来越多地转移到他的慈善礼物上，尤其是在他和妻子同名的慈善事业“陈·扎克伯格倡议”上。
 因此，经过多年的磨合，旧金山医院监事会中的一组医院护士，反Facebook活动家和进步立法者于今年夏天开始动员起来，反对医院的名字。与其决定正式重命名（合同上可能需要归还7500万美元的礼物），该组织决定争取一个中间立场：在保留名称的同时谴责该名称。
 这种愤怒在星期四向政府审计与监督委员会（旧金山委员会监事会）发出。 
“旧金山唯一的公立医院不应以负责危害我们国家乃至世界范围内公共健康的人员的名字命名，但确实如此，”该措施的主要发起人戈登·马说。 “这些是政策选择，而且人数众多。”
 支持我们的另一位主管马特·海尼（Matt Haney）表示：“我们当然感谢这份礼物，也感谢我们在这段时间内给我们最重要的机构的任何礼物。” “但这并不意味着我们应该永远永远放弃这个最重要的公共机构的广告权。”
 会议很快就成为了Facebook创始人的一个弹出式会议，活动家嘲笑他的公司违法行为，并轻视了他的礼物的重要性，这是有史以来最大的给公立医院的私人礼物。这些评论变得很刻薄，例如，一个旧金山人称他为“富有的，不道德的自负主义者，经营着一家采掘公司。”
 同时，慈善领袖对决议可能设立的先例表示关注。高美元慈善事业的捍卫者经常争辩说，不管捐赠为捐赠者提供任何税收优惠或公关赠予，他们的钱对弱势群体也确实有好处。医院在2015年接受了7500万美元的同意，同意将扎克伯格的名字保留至少50年。
 医院基金会负责人金·梅瑞迪斯（Kim Meredith）强调，在冠状病毒大流行期间，扎克伯格和成龙的“衷心礼物”使该市成为“护理的典范”。
 她说：“对[医院]的命名进行谴责的决议有可能带来意想不到的后果，并对过去，现在和将来给这座城市的礼物产生冷淡的影响。” “我们将需要慈善事业来继续应对Covid-19的挑战，健康公平和未来几年的康复。”
 梅雷迪思在后来的声明中补充说，她“为医院现在有了他们的名字而感到骄傲”。

Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg’s adopted hometown of San Francisco took the first step on Thursday to formally condemn the naming of a major hospital after him and his wife, the latest flashpoint in the debate over the proper role for billionaire philanthropy.
 A panel on the city’s board of supervisors recommended  the condemnation, a move that reflects the new, increasingly controversial politics of both the tech industry and of its founders. Activists on both the left and the right have grown sharply critical of big tech companies like Facebook. And simultaneously, there is a building backlash movement to charitable gifts from the mega rich.
 The 3-0 vote is a manifestation of each of those crosscurrents, which both run particularly strong in liberal San Francisco. The measure has no legal force. But a symbolic, full vote of the board is expected on December 15.
 Five years ago, Zuckerberg and his wife, Priscilla Chan, gave $75 million to San Francisco General Hospital, the city’s sole public hospital, where Chan was a pediatrician at the time. As part of the donation, the hospital was formally renamed the Priscilla Chan and Mark Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital and Trauma Center.
 Since then, Zuckerberg has emerged as a political piñata as Facebook grew in size and has been dogged by cascading scandals. And Zuckerberg’s troubles in his corporate life have  increasingly boomeranged onto his charitable gifts, most notably at his and his wife’s eponymous philanthropy, the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.
 And so after years of fits and starts, a group of hospital nurses, anti-Facebook activists, and progressive lawmakers on San Francisco’s board of supervisors began to mobilize this summer to push back against the hospital’s name. Rather than moving to officially rename it — which contractually could require returning the $75 million gift — the group decided to push for a middle ground: to condemn the name while leaving it in place.
 That anger spilled out on Thursday before the Committee on Government Audit and Oversight, a panel on San Francisco’s board of supervisors.
 “San Francisco’s only public hospital should not bear the name of a person responsible for endangering public health in our country and around the world — and yet it does,” said Gordon Mar, the lead sponsor of the measure. “These are policy choices, and they have a body count.”
 “We’re of course thankful for the gift and we’re thankful for any gift to our most important institutions during this time,” said Matt Haney, another supervisor backing it. “But that doesn’t mean that we should for forever essentially have given away advertising rights on this most essential public institution.”
 The session quickly became a pop-off session on the Facebook founder, with activists pillorying him for any corporate transgressions and downplaying the significance of his gift, which was the largest single private gift to a public hospital ever. The comments became quite caustic — for instance, one San Franciscan called him a “rich, amoral egotist who runs an extraction corporation.”
 Meanwhile, charity leaders voiced concern over the precedent that the resolution could set. Defenders of high-dollar philanthropy often argue that regardless of any tax advantages or public-relations boons that the donation offers the giver, their money also does real good for the disadvantaged. And the hospital  agreed in 2015 when it accepted the $75 million that it would keep the Zuckerberg name for at least 50 years.
 Kim Meredith, the head of the hospital’s foundation, stressed that the “heartfelt gift” from Zuckerberg and Chan had made the city “a model of care” during the coronavirus pandemic.
 “This resolution of condemnation on the naming of [the hospital] has the potential of unintended consequences and a chilling effect on past, present, and future gifts to the city,” she said. “We will need philanthropies to continue to tackle the challenges of Covid-19, health equity, and recovery in future years.”
 Meredith added in a later statement that she was “proud that the hospital now bears their names.”
 It is not unusual for an institution to name something after a donor who makes a particularly large gift. Another San Francisco hospital is named after a different tech billionaire, Salesforce founder Marc Benioff. San Francisco officials signed off on the Zuckerberg naming contract in 2015.

note_en:
China's Chang'e-5 mission has returned to Earth with the cargo of rock and "soil" it picked up off the Moon.
 A capsule carrying the materials is reported to have landed in Inner Mongolia shortly after 01:30 local time on Thursday (17:30 GMT, Wednesday).
 It's more than 40 years since the American Apollo and Soviet Luna missions brought their samples home.
 The new specimens should provide fresh insight on the geology and early history of Earth's satellite.
 For China, a successful touchdown will also be seen as another demonstration of its increasing capability in space.
     A probe comprising several elements was sent into orbit around the Moon. These elements then separated, with one half going down to the lunar surface.
 The lander system used a scoop and a drill to dig up samples. It's not clear how much, but possibly in the range of 2-4kg.
 An ascent vehicle subsequently carried the materials back into lunar orbit, where they were transferred to an Earth return module. This is being shepherded home by a fourth element but will detach before executing a fiery descent through Earth's atmosphere.
  Returning from the Moon, the Chang'e-5 module would have been moving much faster than, say, a capsule coming back from the International Space Station.
 Engineers had chosen to scrub some of this extra energy by doing an initial "skip" in the atmosphere. This saw the module briefly dip into the gases that shroud our planet, before then plunging much deeper a second time.
 The Chang'e-5 capsule was targeted to float down on parachute in Siziwang Banner in Inner Mongolia. This is the same location used to bring Chinese astronauts home.
 Ground teams may need some time to locate the capsule because it's winter and there is a lot of snow in Siziwang Banner at the moment.
    A total of just under 400kg of lunar surface materials were picked up by American Apollo astronauts and the Soviets' robotic Luna landers.
 But all these samples were very old - more than three billion years in age. Chang'e-5's rock and dust should be quite different.
 The Chinese mission targeted a high volcanic region called Mons Rümker in the northwest of the nearside of the Moon.
 Samples from this terrain may be no more than 1.2 or 1.3 billion years old, and, as such, should provide additional information on how the Moon is constructed internally.
 The samples will also allow scientists to more precisely calibrate the "chronometer" they use to age surfaces on the inner Solar System planets.
 This is done by counting craters (the more craters, the older the surface), but it depends on having some definitive dating at a number of locations, and the Apollo and Soviet samples were key to this.
   The Moon is once again in vogue. America is planning on returning astronauts to the surface in the middle of this decade. A series of robotic spacecraft will land ahead of these human explorers to do reconnaissance.
 Some these probes will be from national space agencies; some will be sent by commercial enterprises - including from the UK.
 Tony Azzarelli, director and co-founder of the UK industry space body Access Space Alliance, said exciting times lay ahead, and highlighted the start-up Spacebit's quest to put a rover on the lunar surface next year.
 "It'll be the first time that a legged robot will walk on another celestial world. Of course, all of these lunar missions are just a prelude to the return of humans to the Moon in the not-too-distant future," he told BBC News.

Covid-19的最大负担无疑落在了65岁以上的人们身上，约占美国死亡人数的80％。但是，如果我们暂时将其从脑海中淹没，那么其他的东西就会变得显而易见：这种病毒的日冕。
 年轻人正在以历史性的速度死亡。在周三发表在《美国医学协会杂志》上的研究中，我们发现，从3月到7月底，年龄在25至44岁的美国成年人中，死亡人数比根据历史规范预期的死亡人数高出近12,000。
 实际上，7月似乎是现代美国历史上这个年龄段中最致命的月份。在过去的20年中，每年7月平均有11,000名美国年轻人死亡。今年，这一数字激增至16,000多个。
 这种趋势在今年秋天继续。根据先前的趋势，预计到2020年该人口中将有约154,000人死亡。我们在11月中旬超过了这一总数。即使死亡率在12月突然恢复正常（我们知道还没有），我们预计到2020年底，在这一人口中，美国成年人中的死亡人数将超过170,000。
 虽然尚无法提供所有地区的详细数据，但我们知道Covid-19是造成这些过度死亡的动力。考虑纽约州。根据我们从纽约州卫生局收集的统计数据，在4月和5月，Covid-19杀死了1,081名20至49岁的成年人。值得注意的是，这一数字高出该年龄段该州通常的主要死亡原因-意外事故，包括药物过量和道路交通事故-总共导致该人口统计数据在2018年4月和5月期间造成495人死亡，这是该数据的最新年份向公众开放。
 在今年春天东北部恐怖的第一次爆炸消退之后，整个夏天，其他地区也开始出现类似的趋势。随着全国年轻人口病例数量的增加，Covid-19成为其他地区年轻人口死亡的主要原因。尽管今年某些地区年轻人的病毒死亡人数暂时超过了阿片类药物的死亡人数，但我们还担心，在大流行期间，意外的过量用药死亡人数也有所增加。
 有色人种占死亡人数的比例过高也不是一种幻想。根据疾病预防控制中心的数据，截至9月30日，黑人和西班牙裔成年人中25至44岁的成年人不仅占比例不成比例，而且占Covid-19死亡的绝大部分。 
留在家里的政策挽救了生命，但其利益并未平均分配。在必不可少的工人中，其中许多是有色人种，就地庇护从来都不是真正的选择。
 退后一步，比较我们对艾滋病毒/艾滋病流行的经历。在没有有效的治疗方法出现之前，我们惊恐地看着一种传染性强，可预防的疾病ra灭了年轻一代的生命。它每月夺去成千上万劳动年龄成人的生命。虽然仍有太多人被感染，有太多人死亡，但公共卫生信息有助于缓解这一流行病。
 现在，我们必须应对Covid-19。长期以来，我们和我们的同事，政府官员和公众一直在传达这样的信息：Covid-19对老年人是危险的，而年轻人则表现良好。国家卫生统计中心的数据显示，在美国，年龄介于25至44岁之间的成年人死亡的确不到Covid-19死亡人数的3％。
 但是，我们以前对Covid-19在年轻人中相对无害的看法并没有被新兴数据证实。过去，当年轻人开始大量死亡时，我们花了很长时间来应对阿片类药物和艾滋病毒/艾滋病的流行。既然我们有关于Covid-19的类似信息，我们必须立即解决。
 我们现在需要修改消息传递和政策。在未来几周和几个月内必须进行宣传。我们知道它可以提供帮助。在人们对阿片类药物流行的破坏性认识得到普遍了解之后，增加了诸如美沙酮和丁丙诺啡等救生药物的使用，挽救了许多生命。我们需要告诉年轻人，他们处于危险之中，他们需要戴口罩并就社会距离做出更安全的选择。
 既然安全有效的疫苗成为现实，这一点就变得更加重要。年轻健康的人在疫苗推广的优先顺序中处于较低位置。这意味着现在改变行为方式可以在明年挽救成千上万的年轻人的生命。
 这就是关键所在。我们的信息不再仅仅是为了使医院保持畅通而使曲线变平。现在有了疫苗，我们的政策和我们的个人选择可以共同挽救更多的生命。 
杰里米·塞缪尔·福斯特（Jeremy Samuel Faust）是波士顿布里格姆妇女医院急诊科的主治医师，也是哈佛医学院的讲师。 Harlan M. Krumholz是耶鲁大学医学教授。 罗谢尔 P. Walensky ， 首席 传染病 的 马萨诸塞州总医院 的 分工 和 哈佛医学院 的教授 ，一直 由 总统当选人 拜登 提名为 中心疾病 控制和预防 的 主任。  《泰晤士报》致力于向编辑发表各种信件。 我们想听听您对此或我们的任何文章的看法。 这里有一些技巧。 这是我们的电子邮件：信箱@ nytimes.com。
note_en:
The largest burden of Covid-19 has undoubtedly fallen on people older than 65, accounting for around  80 percent of deaths in the United States. But if we momentarily eclipse that from our mind’s eye, something else becomes visible: The corona of this virus.
 Young adults are dying at historic rates. In  research published on Wednesday in the Journal of the American Medical Association, we found that among U.S. adults ages 25 to 44, from March through the end of July, there were almost 12,000 more deaths than were expected based on historical norms.
 In fact, July appears to have been the deadliest month among this age group in modern American history. Over the past 20 years, an average of 11,000 young American adults  died each July. This year that number swelled to over 16,000.
 The trends continued this fall. Based on prior trends, around 154,000 in this demographic had been projected to die in 2020. We surpassed that total in mid-November. Even if death rates suddenly return to normal in December — and we know they have not — we would anticipate well over 170,000 deaths among U.S. adults in this demographic by the end of 2020.
 While detailed data are not yet available for all areas, we know Covid-19 is the driving force behind these excess deaths. Consider New York State. In April and May, Covid-19 killed 1,081 adults ages 20 to 49, according to statistics we gathered from the New York State Health Department. Remarkably, this figure towers over the state’s usual leading cause of death in that age group — unintentional accidents including drug overdoses and road accidents — which combined to cause  495 deaths in this demographic during April and May of 2018, the most recent year for which data are available to the public.
 After the Northeast’s horrific first surge this spring subsided, similar trends began to become apparent in other regions over the summer. As caseloads among the younger population rose nationwide, Covid-19 became a leading cause of death among younger adults in other regions. While deaths from the virus temporarily exceeded opioid deaths among young adults in some areas this year, we are also concerned that unintentional overdose deaths  have increased during the pandemic as well.
 Nor is it an illusion that people of color constitute a disproportionate fraction of the dead. According to the  Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, among adults 25 to 44, Black and Hispanic people make up not just a disproportionate number but a majority of Covid-19 deaths through Sept. 30.
 Stay-at-home policies have saved lives, but their benefits have not been equally distributed. Among essential workers, many of whom are people of color, sheltering-in-place was never a real option.
 Take a step back and compare what we are experiencing to the H.I.V./AIDS epidemic. Before effective treatments became available, we watched in horror as  a contagious but preventable disease ravaged young adults in the prime of their lives. It claimed the lives of thousands of working-age adults every month. While too many still become infected, and too many die, public health messaging helped ease the epidemic.
 Now we must deal with Covid-19. For too long, the message has been repeated — by us and our colleagues, by government officials and the public — that Covid-19 is dangerous for the old and that younger people do well. It’s true that deaths among adults ages 25 to 44 account for fewer than 3 percent of Covid-19 deaths in the United States, according to the National Center for Health Statistics.
 But what we believed before about the relative harmlessness of Covid-19 among younger adults has simply not been borne out by emerging data. In the past, it took us too long to respond to the epidemics of opioids and H.I.V./AIDS when the young started dying in large numbers. Now that we have similar information about Covid-19, we must immediately address it.
 We need to amend our messaging and our policies now. Outreach in the coming weeks and months is imperative. We know it can help. The use of lifesaving  medications like methadone and buprenorphine increased after awareness of the devastation of the opioid epidemic became commonly understood, saving many lives. We need to tell young people that they are at risk and that they need to wear masks and make safer choices about social distancing.
 This is even more important now that safe and effective vaccines are a reality. Young, healthy people are low on the priority list for the vaccine rollout. That means that modifying behavior now can save thousands of young lives next year.
 And that is the crux of it. Our messaging is no longer about merely flattening the curve to prevent hospitals from overflowing. Now with vaccines, our policies and our individual choices can together save a far larger number of lives.
  Jeremy Samuel Faust is an attending physician at Brigham and Women’s Hospital Department of Emergency Medicine in Boston and an instructor at Harvard Medical School. Harlan M. Krumholz is a professor of medicine at Yale University. Rochelle P. Walensky, chief of the division of infectious diseases at Massachusetts General Hospital and a professor at Harvard Medical School, has been nominated by President-elect Biden to be the director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
 The Times is committed to publishing   a diversity of letters  to the editor. We’d like to hear what you think about this or any of our articles. Here are some   tips . And here’s our email:   letters@nytimes.com .
title: 是的，年轻人死于Covid
title_en: Yes, Young People Are Dying of Covid

简介：怀孕期间维生素D缺乏症可能解释了为什么男性被诊断出ASD的可能性是女孩的三倍。这项研究报告说，产前维生素D缺乏会增加发育中的男性大脑中的睾丸激素。
  昆士兰大学的研究人员说，母亲一方缺乏维生素D可以解释为什么自闭症谱系障碍是男孩的三倍。
 昆士兰州昆士兰脑研究所的达里尔·艾尔斯（Darryl Eyles）教授和阿萨德·阿里（Asad Ali）博士在最新研究中发现，怀孕期间维生素D缺乏会导致雄性大鼠大脑中睾丸激素增加。
 “自闭症谱系障碍（ASD）的生物学原因尚不清楚，但我们已经证明，许多风险因素之一-母亲体内的维生素D低-会导致男性胎儿和孕妇血液中睾丸激素的升高。和羊水。”艾尔斯教授说。
 “除了其在钙吸收中的作用外，维生素D对于许多发育过程也至关重要。
 “我们的研究还表明，在缺乏维生素D的男性胎儿中，分解睾丸激素的酶被沉默，可能导致睾丸激素水平升高。”
  艾尔斯教授的先前研究表明，维生素D在大脑发育中起着至关重要的作用，并且在怀孕期间给小鼠补充维生素D可以完全防止其后代出现类似ASD的性状。 
合著者阿里博士说，发育中的大脑过度暴露于诸如睾丸激素之类的性激素被认为是ASD的根本原因，但原因尚不清楚。
 “维生素D参与控制许多性激素的途径，”阿里博士说。
 “当老鼠妈妈喂低维生素D饮食时，它会导致男性胎儿大脑的睾丸激素水平很高。”
 艾尔斯教授说，这项研究是第一个表明已知的ASD危险因素会改变胎儿大脑和母亲血液中的睾丸激素-这可能是ASD在男性中更普遍的原因。
 “我们仅研究了一种自闭症的危险因素-发育过程中维生素D缺乏-我们的下一步是研究其他可能的危险因素，例如产妇压力和缺氧-缺氧-看看它们是否具有相同的作用，”他说。
 资金资助：这是与西澳大利亚大学的安德鲁·怀特豪斯（Andrew Whitehouse）博士的合作，由澳大利亚国家卫生与医学研究委员会和昆士兰州精神健康研究中心资助。
  资料来源：昆士兰大学联系人：简·伊尔斯利–昆士兰大学图片：图片属于公共领域 
原始研究：开放访问。 Asad Amanat Ali，Xiaoying Cui，Renata Aparecida Nedel Pertile，Xiang Li，Gregory Medley，Suzanne Adele Alexander，Andrew J.O. Whitehouse和John Joseph McGrath撰写的“发育中的维生素D缺乏症会增加胎儿对睾丸激素的暴露”。达里尔·沃尔特·艾尔斯（Darryl Walter Eyles）。分子自闭症
      自闭症谱系障碍（ASD）是一组神经发育障碍，在男性中更为常见。 “产前性类固醇”假说将胎儿生命中过量的性类固醇暴露与ASD中观察到的行为差异联系起来。但是，性类固醇暴露可能过多的原因仍不清楚。流行病学研究确定了与ASD相关的几种环境危险因素，包括发育性维生素D（DVD）缺乏。我们已经在动物模型中证明DVD缺陷与雄性而非雌性胎盘的胎盘中的过度炎症反应有关。维生素D还可以调节体外几种类固醇生成酶的表达。因此，使用这种动物模型，我们检查了DVD缺陷是否会导致母体和胎儿区室中的性类固醇水平升高。
  从交配前的第6周开始直到雌性大鼠在胚胎第18天收集组织，雌性大鼠都要接受缺乏维生素D的饮食。我们检查了母体血浆，胎脑和羊水中睾丸激素，雄烯二酮和皮质酮的水平。我们进一步检查了胎儿脑中类固醇生成酶的基因表达和芳香化酶启动子的DNA甲基化，作为调节睾丸激素表达的潜在分子机制。
  我们表明，DVD缺乏症会增加孕妇血液中的睾丸激素水平。我们还显示女性（而非男性）缺乏DVD的胎儿羊水中的睾丸激素和雄烯二酮水平升高。 DVD缺陷型男性大脑中的睾丸激素水平也升高。像其他类固醇相关激素一样，维生素D通过甲基化调节基因表达。因此，我们检查了男性大脑中睾丸激素的显着升高是否归因于这种潜在的基因沉默机制。我们显示，与男性对照相比，芳香化酶的启动子是高度甲基化的。
  芳香化酶的减少，除了会引起过多的睾丸激素外，还可能导致雌二醇的减少，这在此未进行检查。
  这项研究是第一个显示流行病学上确定的ASD环境危险因素如何选择性升高男性胚胎脑中睾丸激素的方法。这些发现为ASD的产前性类固醇理论提供了进一步的机制支持。
 这些变量能否在人类中具有与退化X染色体相关的相对模式？以及与女性有关，后来的“ D”降解不仅是骨质疏松症（因出生而引起）的病因，而且也是睾丸激素的更年期增加的原因。（以及男孩生几胎后的衰老作用？，而不是两种性别的多种妊娠……修辞但理论上的询问（“！？。”）

Summary: Vitamin D deficiency during pregnancy may explain why males are three times more likely to be diagnosed with ASD than girls. The study reports prenatal vitamin d deficiency increases testosterone in the developing male brain.
  A deficiency in Vitamin D on the mother’s side could explain why Autism spectrum disorder is three times more common in boys, say researchers from The University of Queensland.
 In their latest study, Professor Darryl Eyles and Dr Asad Ali from UQ’s Queensland Brain Institute found vitamin D deficiency during pregnancy caused an increase in testosterone in the developing brain of male rats.
 “The biological cause of Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is unknown but we have shown that one of the many risk factors–low vitamin D in mothers–causes an increase in testosterone in the brain of the male foetuses, as well as the maternal blood and amniotic fluid,” Professor Eyles said.
 “In addition to its role in calcium absorption, vitamin D is crucial to many developmental processes.
 “Our research also showed that in vitamin D-deficient male foetuses, an enzyme which breaks down testosterone was silenced and could be contributing to the presence of high testosterone levels.”
  Professor Eyles’ previous research has shown that vitamin D plays a critical role in brain development and that giving vitamin D supplements to mice during pregnancy completely prevented ASD-like traits in their offspring.
 Co-author Dr Ali said that excessive exposure of the developing brain to sex hormones like testosterone was thought to be an underlying cause of ASD, but the reasons remained unclear.
 “Vitamin D is involved in pathways controlling many sex hormones,” Dr Ali said.
 “When the rat mothers were fed a low vitamin D diet, it caused male foetal brains to have high levels of exposure to testosterone.”
 Professor Eyles said the study was the first to show that a known risk factor for ASD alters testosterone in both the foetal brain and the mother’s blood — one possible contributor to why ASD is more prevalent in males.
 “We have only studied one risk factor for ASD — vitamin D deficiency during development — our next step is to look at other possible risk factors, such as maternal stress and hypoxia – lack of oxygen – and see if they have the same effect,” he said.
 Funding: It is a collaboration with The University of Western Australia’s Dr Andrew Whitehouse and funded by the National Health and Medical Research Council Australia and Queensland Centre for Mental Health Research.
  Source:  University of Queensland  Contact: Jane Ilsley – University of Queensland  Image: The image is in the public domain
 Original Research: Open access. “ Developmental vitamin D deficiency increases foetal exposure to testosterone” by Asad Amanat Ali, Xiaoying Cui, Renata Aparecida Nedel Pertile, Xiang Li, Gregory Medley, Suzanne Adele Alexander, Andrew J. O. Whitehouse, John Joseph McGrath & Darryl Walter Eyles.  Molecular Autism
      Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is a group of neurodevelopmental disorders which are more common in males. The ‘prenatal sex steroid’ hypothesis links excessive sex-steroid exposure during foetal life with the behavioural differences observed in ASD. However, the reason why sex steroid exposure may be excessive remains unclear. Epidemiological studies have identified several environmental risk factors associated with ASD, including developmental vitamin D (DVD) deficiency. We have demonstrated in an animal model that DVD-deficiency is associated with a hyper-inflammatory response in placentas from male but not female foetuses. Vitamin D also regulates the expression of several steroidogenic enzymes in vitro. Therefore using this animal model, we have examined whether DVD-deficiency leads to increased sex-steroid levels in both the maternal and foetal compartments.
  Female rats are fed a vitamin D deficient diet from 6 weeks before mating until tissue collection at embryonic day 18. We examined the levels of testosterone, androstenedione and corticosterone in maternal plasma, foetal brains and amniotic fluid. We further examined gene expressions of steroidogenic enzymes and DNA methylation of aromatase promoters in foetal brains as a potential molecular mechanism regulating testosterone expression.
  We show that DVD-deficiency increases testosterone levels in maternal blood. We also show elevated levels of testosterone and androstenedione in the amniotic fluid of female but not male DVD-deficient foetuses. Testosterone levels were also elevated in DVD-deficient male brains. Vitamin D, like other steroid-related hormones, regulates gene expression via methylation. Therefore we examined whether the significant elevation in testosterone in male brains was due to such a potential gene-silencing mechanism. We show that the promoter of aromatase was hyper-methylated compared to male controls.
  A reduction in aromatase, in addition to causing excessive testosterone, could also lead to a reduction in estradiol which was not examined here.
  This study is the first to show how an epidemiologically established environmental risk factor for ASD may selectively elevate testosterone in male embryonic brains. These findings provide further mechanistic support for the prenatal sex steroid theory of ASD.

 之后，您可以启动游戏并开始玩。大多数游戏都需要游戏手柄，因此您可能还希望将游戏手柄与iPhone或iPad配对。

 尽管您不必支付额外的订阅费即可玩这些游戏，但是您可以选择成为Stadia Pro的订阅者。除了您在平台上购买的游戏外，它还允许您访问游戏库并解锁4K视频。 Stadia Pro每月收费9.99美元。

note_en:
If you want to try it out with a free or paid Stadia account, you can head over to  stadia.google.com from your iOS device. Log in to your Google account, add a shortcut to your home screen and open the web app.
 After that, you can launch a game and start playing. Most games will require a gamepad, so you might want to pair a gamepad with your iPhone or iPad as well.
  Apple’s iOS supports Xbox One and PlayStation 4 controllers using Bluetooth as well as controllers specifically designed for iOS. You can also play with the Stadia controller, but it’s optional. If you just want to check your inventory quickly, Stadia on iOS also supports touch controls.
 Stadia works a bit like a console that runs in the cloud. You have to buy games for the platform specifically and you can then stream them from a data center near you. Recent additions include Cyberpunk 2077 and Assassin’s Creed Valhalla.
 While you don’t have to pay an additional subscription to play those games, you can optionally become a Stadia Pro subscribers. In addition to games you bought on the platform, it lets you access a library of games and it unlocks 4K video. Stadia Pro costs $9.99 per month.
 In other Stadia news, earlier this week, Ubisoft  announced that you could subscribe to the company’s unlimited subscription service Ubisoft+ and access games from Stadia. For now, it’s only available as a beta in the U.S.
title: Google Stadia现在可在iOS上使用

 2020年让我们中的许多人对发生的一切大加鞭打！特朗普总统在推特上表示他将中止向美国移民后，我们在4月做好了最坏的准备。最后，他在4月和6月发布的行政公告远没有达到这个水平，并且仍有可能移民。
 但是，这些禁令至少在2020年底之前一直有效。该公告暂停了美国大使馆和驻海外领事馆以及H-1B，H-2B，J-1和L- 1工作签证。国务院扩大了这些禁令的例外清单，现在有许多人符合条件。 
当前影响大多数人的制约因素之一是，许多使馆和领事馆仍处于关闭状态或工作能力大大降低。 鉴于此，我们建议已在美国的客户避免移民，方法是向USCIS寻求身份扩展，身份变更和身份调整。  H-1B可能是您作为创始人的未来的另一种有前途的签证选择。 有两种方法：“上限对象”（年度春季彩票）和“上限豁免”（一年中的任何时间）。 至少，对于您的初创公司来说，很容易在2021年3月为您注册即将到来的H-1B彩票。注册H-1B候选人只需10美元。 如果您被选中，您的初创公司可以代表您提交H-1B申请。 如果未选择您，则启动公司可以在2022年再次为您注册。
note_en:
Sophie Alcorn is the founder of  Alcorn Immigration Law in Silicon Valley and 2019 Global Law Experts Awards’ “Law Firm of the Year in California for Entrepreneur Immigration Services.” She connects people with the businesses and opportunities that expand their lives.
  “Your questions are vital to the spread of knowledge that allows people all over the world to rise above borders and pursue their dreams,” says  Sophie Alcorn, a Silicon Valley immigration attorney. “Whether you’re in people ops, a founder or seeking a job in Silicon Valley, I would love to  answer your questions in my next column.”
 Extra Crunch members receive access to weekly “Dear Sophie” columns;  use promo code ALCORN to purchase a one- or two-year subscription for 50% off.
   I’m on an F1 OPT and am about to incorporate a startup with my two American co-founders. What were the biggest immigration changes in 2020 affecting us?
    Congrats on creating your startup. The Electoral College has voted and Biden is scheduled to take office on January 20, 2021. It may take him a few months to undo many of the Trump immigration changes, so there are several things for you to consider.
 2020 gave many of us whiplash with all the things that happened! We braced for the worst in April after President Trump tweeted that he would suspend immigration to the U.S. In the end, the executive proclamations he issued in April and June fell far short of that and immigration remains possible.
 However, these bans remain in effect until at least the end of 2020. The proclamations placed moratoriums on the issuance of green cards by the U.S. Embassies and consulates abroad, as well as H-1B, H-2B, J-1 and L-1 work visas. The Department of State has expanded the list of exceptions to these bans so many people now qualify.
 One of the current constraints affecting the most people is that many embassies and consulates remain closed or are operating at significantly reduced capacity. Given that, we are recommending to our clients who are already in the U.S. to avoid leaving by seeking Extensions of Status, Changes of Status and Adjustments of Status with USCIS stateside.
 The H-1B may be another promising visa option for your future as a founder. There are two ways to do it: “cap-subject” (the annual spring lottery) and “cap-exempt” (anytime of year). At a minimum, it’s easy for your startup to register you for the upcoming H-1B lottery in March 2021. It only costs $10 to register an  H-1B candidate. If you’re selected, your startup could file an H-1B petition on your behalf. If you are not selected, your startup can register you again in 2022.
title: 亲爱的索菲：2020年，初创企业创始人的移民情况如何变化？
title_en: Dear Sophie: How did immigration change for startup founders in 2020?
